# Haka Decks

> Haka Decks is a custom deck builder in Centennial, Colorado, serving the south Denver metro. We design and build composite and hardwood decks, pergolas, patio covers, outdoor kitchens, and railings — engineered for Colorado's altitude, sun, and freeze-thaw.

Haka Decks (formerly Haka Construction) is a service-first deck shop founded in 2017 by Pete Borlase. We hold a 5.0-star rating across 87 Google reviews. Every build is permitted, code-compliant, and backed by a craftsmanship guarantee — we don't stop until the homeowner is happy.

## Services

- [Composite Decks](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/composite-decks): Low-maintenance, high-durability composite decking engineered for Colorado's altitude, hail, and freeze-thaw — our default recommendation for most south Denver homes. Built as a Deckorators Pro Elite installer, Trex Platinum Pro, and TimberTech Authorized Dealer.
- [Hardwood & Cedar Decks](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/hardwood-cedar-decks): Real-wood warmth for homeowners who want natural grain — clear-grade cedar and premium hardwoods, built to last in Colorado's climate.
- [Pergolas & Patio Covers](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/pergolas-patio-covers): Year-round shade and shelter, from open-rafter pergolas to fully roofed pavilions and screened enclosures, engineered end to end for Colorado weather.
- [Outdoor Kitchens & Bars](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/outdoor-kitchens): Built-in grills, ventilated counters, sinks, and refrigeration — engineered into the deck structure and detailed to survive Colorado winters.
- [Railing Systems](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/railings): Cable, aluminum, glass-panel, or composite railing — code-compliant on every Colorado deck.
- [Deck Repair & Restoration](https://www.hakadeck.com/services/deck-repair): If the bones are good, we save the structure and replace the surface; if they're not, we'll tell you straight.

## Service area

Centennial, Greenwood Village, Cherry Hills Village, Lone Tree, Highlands Ranch, Castle Rock, and the greater south Denver metro, Colorado.

## Materials & certifications

- Deckorators Certified Pro Elite Installer
- Trex Platinum Pro contractor
- TimberTech Authorized Dealer
- Also installs Fortress, Westbury, AZEK, Fiberon, and Wolf decking and railing systems
- Natural options: clear-grade cedar and thermally-modified hardwood

## Frequently asked questions

- **What materials do you use?** Primarily composite (Deckorators is the go-to, with Trex and TimberTech), plus clear-grade cedar and thermally-modified hardwood. Composite stands up to Colorado freeze-thaw, hail, and altitude UV, with 25–50 year manufacturer warranties.
- **How long does a deck project take?** Most residential decks take 3–10 days of on-site build time once we break ground. Permit and HOA approvals typically run 2–4 weeks before that. Larger covered or multi-tier builds run 2–4 weeks on site.
- **How much does a new deck cost?** A typical 300–400 sq ft composite deck in the Denver area runs $15,000–$30,000 fully installed, including stairs, railing, and permits. Covered structures, outdoor kitchens, and multi-level designs cost more.
- **Will a deck increase my home's value?** Yes — a composite deck addition recoups roughly 80% of its cost at resale (Remodeling Magazine Cost vs. Value), and finished outdoor living spaces help south Denver homes sell faster.
